name: Phelps County Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank with 100% employee ownership, operating multiple branches in Rolla and St. James, Missouri since 1963.
description_long:
Phelps County Bank was established in 1963 and operates as a fully FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank is uniquely structured as a 100% employee-owned organization through an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP), meaning all employees are stakeholders in the company's success. This ownership model reflects their stated mission to build relationships with purpose, integrity, and fairness through commitment to teamwork, education, and growth.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of consumer and business banking services including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, youth savings products, money market accounts, and online account opening capabilities. They provide mortgage and home equity lending with options for 100% financing, construction loans, land purchases, and personal/business loans. Additional services include investment advisory through the PCB Investment Center, a mobile banking app for iPhone and Android, online bill payment, e-statements, and ATM access across multiple locations.

Phelps County Bank distinguishes itself through genuine community integration and employee ownership alignment. Their headquarters and primary hub is located in the historic Edwin Long Hotel in downtown Rolla, established in 1963. The bank operates three branches in Rolla (Pine Street downtown location, Highway 72 location, and Missouri S&T campus location) plus a location in St. James, with extended drive-thru hours (until 6 PM weekdays) and Saturday banking availability. They actively engage in community giving through an employee-owner donation program and maintain a presence on social media.

For consumers seeking traditional community banking, Phelps County Bank provides legitimate deposit insurance, established local presence, and multiple service channels. However, as a regional bank with limited geographic footprint, customers outside Missouri will have no physical branch access. The bank's website does not prominently display current interest rates for deposits or loans, requiring customers to contact them directly for rate information. Compared to national banks, online features and digital innovation appear more basic, though adequate for traditional banking needs. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Online account opening for deposit products
Home purchase mortgages with 100% financing options
Investment advisory services through PCB Investment Center
Online banking with bill payment and e-statements
pros: 100% FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Genuine 100% employee ownership through ESOP structure aligns bank incentives with customer service
Established since 1963 with deep community roots in Phelps County
Multiple convenient branch locations with extended drive-thru hours (7:30 AM - 6:00 PM weekdays)
Free personal checking accounts and free minor savings products available
Online account opening for checking and savings accounts available 24/7
Active community giving program with employee-owner donation requests available
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Missouri (Rolla, St. James, and Springfield only) with no national branch network
Interest rates for deposits and loans not displayed on website—customers must call or visit to learn rates
Digital banking platform appears basic compared to national banks; mobile app mentioned but limited details provided
No prominent information about overdraft fees, ATM fees, or other banking charges disclosed on website
Limited online loan application details; mortgage application mentions '15 minutes' but other loan processes unclear
best_for: Missouri residents in Phelps County (Rolla, St. James area) seeking relationship-based community banking
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance safety and employee-owned business alignment over digital convenience
Small business owners and farmers needing local relationship lending and business accounts
Homebuyers seeking mortgage options including 100% financing and construction/land purchase loans
diagnosis: Phelps County Bank is best suited for Missouri-based consumers and small businesses in Phelps County who value community relationships, employee ownership alignment, and FDIC safety. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank serves only a small regional area in Missouri and offers no national presence, making it unsuitable for customers outside the service territory or those requiring extensive digital banking features.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
